formula: rank(ts_std(amount / circ_market_cap, 60) / ts_std(amount / circ_market_cap, 20)) * rank(-ts_max(turnover_rate,20) / ts_sum(turnover_rate,20))
name: float_compress_even_turnover_20_60

formula: rank(-ts_corr(ret_1d, shift(ret_1d, 1), 20)) * rank(ts_mean(amount / circ_market_cap, 20) / ts_max(amount / circ_market_cap, 20))
name: anti_autocorr_floatflow_evenness_20

formula: rank(-ts_corr(ret_1d, shift(ret_1d, 1), 20)) * rank(ts_min(open / close, 20) / ts_max(open / close, 20))
name: anti_autocorr_openclose_corridor_20

formula: rank(-ts_corr(ret_1d, shift(ret_1d, 1), 20)) * rank(ts_mean(amount / circ_market_cap, 20) / ts_max(amount / circ_market_cap, 20))
name: anti_autocorr_float_peak_balance_20

formula: rank(ts_std(amount / circ_market_cap,60) / ts_std(amount / circ_market_cap,20)) * rank(-ts_mean(high / low - 1,10))
name: float_compress_absorb_20_60

formula: rank(-ts_corr(ret_1d, shift(ret_1d, 1), 20)) * rank(ts_min(amount / circ_market_cap, 20) / ts_mean(amount / circ_market_cap, 20))
name: anti_autocorr_floatflow_floor_20
anti_autocorr_float_absorb_20
mispricing_source: Quiet float-normalized absorption is harder for attention-driven participants to price than bursty price moves; the anti-autocorr leg avoids crowded momentum tapes and focuses on hidden accumulation rather than breakout chasing.
forced_counterparty: Retail momentum chasers and liquidity-motivated sellers from rebalancing/unlock/exit flow who sell into steady bids without optimizing for medium-horizon alpha.
failure_mode: Fails in speculative trend regimes where breakout continuation dominates mean reversion, or when float data is distorted by corporate actions and no longer reflects true tradable supply.

name: anti_autocorr_turnover_corridor_20
mispricing_source: Slow underreaction to quiet accumulation; noisy retail prints mask the inventory transfer until stronger hands finish absorbing supply.
forced_counterparty: Retail chasers and T+1 constrained exit sellers; secondarily benchmarked funds trading for liquidity rather than alpha.
failure_mode: Fails in momentum-chasing/speculative regimes where return autocorr turns positive or turnover spikes break the corridor.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_volume_floorstd_20
mispricing_source: Underreaction to persistent sponsored participation masked by noisy daily mean reversion; distinct from surge-style volume factors because it rewards non-collapsing weekly flow support rather than bursts
forced_counterparty: short-horizon retail churn traders, benchmark/rebalance flows, and liquidity-motivated sellers
data_requirements: ["ret_1d","volume"]; intraday order-flow or member-level trade ledger is still needed before money-ready deployment to verify true absorption
failure_mode: Fails when a high volume floor reflects crowding/distribution or one-way speculative momentum instead of informed sponsorship

name: low_lottery_log_amount_smooth_20_5
mispricing_source: Attention-driven lottery demand overpays for recent one-day pops; this factor instead rewards quiet flow regularity, so it is not just another price-momentum variant.
forced_counterparty: Retail momentum chasers and benchmarked managers reacting to theme/limit-up activity instead of quietly accumulated names.
failure_mode: Fails when policy/thematic rallies reward lottery spikes directly, or when institutional accumulation turns bursty so smooth amount paths no longer separate informed flow from inactivity.

name: anti_autocorr_amount_evenness_20
mispricing_source: Patient buyers absorb episodic retail and liquidity-driven selling without needing breakout volume, so the market underreacts until available supply is exhausted.
forced_counterparty: Retail momentum/lottery traders and liquidity-motivated sellers from redemption or benchmark rebalancing
failure_mode: Fails in broad one-way momentum regimes or when even amount mostly reflects passive/index churn rather than informed accumulation.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_amount_change_stability_20
mispricing_source: underreaction to gradual capital absorption; this targets smoothness of flow changes rather than static liquidity level, so it is not just another plain amount-stability clone
forced_counterparty: retail attention traders and benchmark-constrained mutual funds reacting to headlines, subscriptions, and redemption flow rather than optimizing entry timing
failure_mode: breaks when the market shifts into one-way speculative momentum and bursty turnover is rewarded instead of penalized

name: crowded_glamour_turnover_instability_20
mispricing_source: Retail attention and benchmark-aware growth chasing overpay for narrative-heavy names; unstable participation reveals fragile ownership rather than informed accumulation.
forced_counterparty: retail momentum chasers and benchmark-constrained managers who cannot easily avoid crowded glamour stocks
failure_mode: Works poorly in broad momentum/speculation regimes where expensive crowded stocks keep attracting incremental capital instead of mean-reverting.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_floatflow_smooth_20
mispricing_source: Slow ownership transfer hidden in smooth float-adjusted flow, not a simple past-price signal; the edge should be less correlated to pure momentum/reversal because it keys off flow path stability per unit of tradable float.
forced_counterparty: Retail churn and redemption/benchmark-constrained sellers providing stock mechanically, while institutions are execution-constrained and must slice orders over many days.
failure_mode: Stops working when the tape becomes one-way momentum instead of mean-reverting, or when float-adjusted flow smoothness becomes crowded and no longer reflects private-information absorption.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_turnover_evenness_20
mispricing_source: Slow institutional accumulation under participation constraints versus noisy retail attention that concentrates turnover into chase days; the market underprices the smoother demand path.
forced_counterparty: Retail momentum traders and benchmarked/redeeming funds that must trade around flows rather than alpha
failure_mode: Stops working when policy/news shocks dominate microstructure and turnover becomes regime-wide synchronized, making weekly evenness no longer informative.

name: anti_autocorr_turnover_floor_20
mispricing_source: Retail-dominated short-horizon overreaction plus VWAP/benchmark-constrained institutional buying slows incorporation of information into prices.
forced_counterparty: Retail theme traders and benchmarked funds forced to trade steadily rather than wait for ideal entry points
failure_mode: Breakout/event regimes where returns become positively autocorrelated, or when a steady turnover floor reflects illiquid stagnation instead of real absorption.

name: turnover_floor_body_stability_20
mispricing_source: underreaction to quiet institutional accumulation hidden inside non-explosive daily price action
forced_counterparty: retail liquidity sellers and benchmark/rebalancing funds trading for mandate or cash-flow reasons rather than alpha
failure_mode: breaks in speculative theme-chasing regimes where high turnover floors and tight bodies reflect crowding or price-limit behavior instead of accumulation

name: value_weekly_turnover_change_stability_20
mispricing_source: Neglected value names with smooth participation changes reflect gradual ownership transfer and delayed fundamental re-rating, rather than crowded momentum.
forced_counterparty: Retail lottery chasers and benchmark-constrained long-only funds that cannot aggressively add unpopular cheap names before confirmation
data_requirements: ["pb","turnover_rate"] from daily data; derived 5d pct_change and 20d ts_std only
failure_mode: Fails when speculative junk-led rallies dominate or when low-PB names are true value traps and smooth turnover just reflects structural neglect

name: anti_autocorr_float_floor_20
mispricing_source: steady float-normalized absorption is slow to enter prices because daily reversal noise masks institutional accumulation
forced_counterparty: retail traders and benchmark-constrained sellers exiting dull names into stronger narratives
failure_mode: breaks in momentum-chasing or liquidation regimes where high turnover floors reflect crowding/distribution instead of patient absorption

name: turnover_absret_corr_20
mispricing_source: Transient crowding and impact from volatility-sensitive trading flow; this is a flow-reactivity signal, not a raw price-volatility clone.
forced_counterparty: Retail attention traders and benchmark-constrained funds reacting to news/liquidity bursts
failure_mode: Breaks in strong momentum/news regimes where turnover chasing reflects real information instead of temporary crowding

name: anti_autocorr_float_peak_balance_20
mispricing_source: A-share retail flow and T+1 churn create temporary overhangs; distributed float turnover plus weak return feedback signals quiet absorption, not a raw price-trend variant.
forced_counterparty: retail theme chasers and liquidity-motivated sellers/rebalancers who trade on urgency rather than expected alpha
failure_mode: Fails in tape-driven momentum or policy-beta melt-up regimes where one-day participation spikes and positive feedback become rewarded instead of penalized.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_relvol_floor_20
mispricing_source: Attention-driven underreaction to orderly sponsorship; this is a participation-regularity signal rather than a raw price-momentum clone.
forced_counterparty: retail momentum churn and benchmark/rebalance flow traders
failure_mode: Fails in strong speculative momentum regimes where a high relvol floor reflects crowding/leadership instead of quiet absorption, or when anti-autocorrelation collapses.

name: reflexive_turnover_value_20
mispricing_source: This is a return-flow reflexivity sleeve, not a plain price factor: it isolates stocks where trading intensity mechanically chases prior returns, then filters for names already priced expensively on book value.
forced_counterparty: Retail momentum chasers and benchmark-sensitive long-only managers who crowd into recent liquid winners and under-own cheap laggards for career-risk and liquidity reasons.
data_requirements: ["ret_5d","turnover_rate","pb"] from CN daily price + daily_basic; no non-daily data required
failure_mode: It should weaken in strong policy-driven momentum regimes where expensive crowded winners keep attracting flows longer than the 10-20 day horizon and valuation stops anchoring price.

name: weekly_relvol_evenness_underreaction_20
mispricing_source: Retail overreaction creates short-horizon reversals, while steady weekly participation reflects slower institutional accumulation; price underreacts because the flow quality is not obvious from raw returns alone.
forced_counterparty: retail liquidity-demand traders and benchmarked funds handling subscription/redemption flow
failure_mode: Fails when relative volume is dominated by theme-chasing or marketwide policy shocks, so even participation no longer distinguishes accumulation from crowding.

name: low_lottery_turnover_evenness_20
mispricing_source: Retail attention in A-shares overpays for recent jackpot days, while steady multi-day participation is underreacted to over a 10-20 day horizon.
forced_counterparty: institutions with impact constraints must spread execution across days; retail attention-chasers and news-driven liquidity demanders take the other side around spikes
failure_mode: Fails in strong chasing regimes where recent upside spikes keep trending and turnover concentration reflects real information instead of speculative crowding.

name: weekly_turnover_corridor_20
mispricing_source: Slow diffusion of patient accumulation; this is a participation-evenness sleeve, not a high-correlation variant of a price trend factor.
forced_counterparty: Redemption-driven sellers and benchmark-constrained long-only funds that cannot fully pre-position, plus retail liquidity suppliers who exit quiet names too early.
failure_mode: It should weaken when policy/speculative manias favor bursty turnover names, or if the signal collapses into a generic liquidity proxy.

name: weekly_float_body_evenness_20
mispricing_source: Underreaction to patient float-normalized accumulation; this keys off flow evenness and candle-body noisiness, not raw momentum or a plain liquidity level.
forced_counterparty: Benchmarked mutual funds and index/rebalance flow that must trade over multiple days, with retail attention-chasers taking the other side around noisier names.
failure_mode: Breaks in policy/thematic chase regimes or price-limit environments where tight bodies are mechanical and bursty participation carries genuine information.

name: weekly_relvol_stability_20
mispricing_source: delayed price response to orderly sponsorship and gradual ownership transfer
forced_counterparty: retail theme/attention traders and benchmark or redemption-driven sellers trading for liquidity rather than medium-horizon alpha
failure_mode: Fails when speculative regimes reward bursty participation, or when stable relative volume reflects stagnant illiquidity instead of informed accumulation.

name: value_low_lottery_20
mispricing_source: Retail preference for glamour/high-skew names plus institutional career risk around boring cheap stocks; the payoff is neglect and taste-driven mispricing, not raw momentum/reversal reuse.
forced_counterparty: retail lottery seekers and benchmark-constrained long-only managers
failure_mode: Fails in speculative momentum regimes when expensive high-skew names keep attracting flows and valuation mean reversion is delayed.

name: log_amount_change_stability_20_5
mispricing_source: This is a trading-value path signal, not a price-direction clone; smooth amount-change paths indicate orderly inventory transfer and underreaction after temporary flow shocks.
forced_counterparty: retail theme chasers and liquidity-needy holders trading in bursts around headlines
failure_mode: Fails in true information or policy-breakout regimes when bursty amount reflects real fundamental repricing and continuation, not noise.

name: weekly_float_floorstd_20
mispricing_source: This captures persistence of float-normalized participation, not past returns or raw price trend; slow ownership transfer is mispriced because dull, steady flow attracts less attention than spike days.
forced_counterparty: Retail attention/lottery traders and benchmark- or cash-flow-constrained funds rotating for non-alpha reasons
failure_mode: Fails in policy/thematic momentum regimes where bursty turnover is rewarded, or when passive/index flow dominates float turnover so steady support no longer indicates informed accumulation.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_turnover_floorstd_20
mispricing_source: Regular participation support plus short-horizon reversal is a flow-quality signal, not a raw price or burst-volume signal; it should be less redundant than simple momentum/reversal or one-day price-volume factors.
forced_counterparty: retail attention-chasers, benchmark/rebalance-constrained funds, and liquidity-motivated sellers
data_requirements: ["ret_1d","turnover_rate"]; intraday order-flow or member-level trade ledger is still required before money-ready deployment to verify true absorption vs crowding.
failure_mode: Fails in strong trend/chasing regimes where returns turn positively autocorrelated, or when a high turnover floor reflects crowded speculation/distribution rather than patient institutional accumulation.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_relvol_20
mispricing_source: Attention-driven flow is over-extrapolated and shorting is constrained, so non-persistent relative-volume shocks can leave behind underpriced names after transient crowding clears; this is a flow/attention sleeve, not a price-factor variant.
forced_counterparty: retail attention traders and benchmarked managers reacting mechanically to headline volume
failure_mode: Fails in genuine information-breakout regimes where persistent relative-volume expansion is informed and should be followed instead of faded.

name: turnover_floorstd_underreaction_20
mispricing_source: The payoff comes from underreaction to quiet but persistent sponsorship; this is a participation-quality signal, not a raw price-momentum clone.
forced_counterparty: Retail momentum/liquidity traders and benchmark-constrained funds trading around subscriptions, redemptions, and rebalance flow rather than medium-horizon alpha
failure_mode: Fails in breakout or policy-beta regimes where positive return autocorrelation is rewarded, or when a high turnover floor reflects crowding/distribution instead of patient accumulation

name: low_lottery_weekly_float_floorstd_20
mispricing_source: Lottery preference and attention bias push capital toward volatile names, leaving quiet free-float absorption underreacted to until positions get marked up.
forced_counterparty: Retail lottery chasers and non-alpha liquidity/rebalance sellers.
failure_mode: Fails in momentum-frenzy regimes where unstable high-spike names keep winning and steady float absorption no longer carries a scarcity premium.

name: weekly_turnover_floorstd_20
mispricing_source: The payoff comes from stable ownership structure and slow-moving capital; this is a liquidity-shape signal, not another price-trend clone.
forced_counterparty: Retail/speculative traders and liquidity-motivated sellers who rotate out of low-excitement names and leave steady accumulators to absorb supply
failure_mode: Fails when speculative high-churn regimes dominate and turnover spikes are rewarded more than stable participation floors

name: float_even_underreaction_20
mispricing_source: Quiet float turnover is a flow/ownership signal, and the market underreacts when that flow is not accompanied by an immediate 5-day price move; this is a flow-response inefficiency, not a high-correlation price-only variant.
forced_counterparty: liquidity-needing retail sellers and redemption-constrained small funds rotating out on weak recent price action
failure_mode: It should stop working in policy/news-driven momentum regimes where flow spikes become genuinely informative immediately, or if float-normalized flow collapses into a large-cap liquidity proxy.

name: weekly_amount_tail_evenness_20
mispricing_source: Slow-moving capital must slice orders across days, creating orderly amount-change paths that the market underreacts to; this is a flow-shape signal on amount increments, not a high-correlation price-path variant.
forced_counterparty: Benchmark-aware mutual funds and other participation-capped institutions; retail attention traders who overpay for bursty turnover elsewhere
failure_mode: Fails when speculative bursty-liquidity regimes keep being rewarded, or when mechanical ETF/index rebalance flow dominates amount without informational content.

name: weekly_turnover_compress_autocorr_20_60
mispricing_source: Order-splitting and T+1/retail attention frictions make information diffuse over 10-20 days; compressed weekly turnover suggests supply is being absorbed without broad speculative participation.
forced_counterparty: benchmarked/redeeming funds and liquidity-needing retail holders who trade for mandate or cash-flow reasons rather than alpha
failure_mode: Fails in broad speculative bursts or policy-driven tape shifts where turnover compression no longer signals absorption and short-term return autocorrelation flips sign.

name: weekly_float_flow_floor_20
mispricing_source: Underreaction to persistent float-normalized accumulation; this is a participation-floor signal, not a raw price or volume-spike variant.
forced_counterparty: impatient retail sellers and redemption-driven holders
failure_mode: Stops working if passive/index rebalances or policy-event bursts dominate flow, or if the market shifts to spike-chasing momentum where steady sponsorship is ignored.

name: anti_autocorr_float_floorstd_20
mispricing_source: Float-normalized baseline participation captures non-price-sensitive accumulation; the anti-autocorr leg filters for names where tape noise still masks that sponsorship, so this is not just another price-only reversal factor.
forced_counterparty: T+1 retail churners and benchmark-constrained funds meeting rebalances or redemptions
failure_mode: Fails when stable float turnover is just illiquidity instead of sponsorship, or when the market shifts into a momentum regime where positive autocorr reflects genuine revaluation

name: turnover_floor_underreaction_20
mispricing_source: Slow institutional accumulation in liquid names meets short-horizon retail overreaction; the turnover floor captures steady sponsorship, and anti-autocorr captures temporary inventory-driven reversals that mean-revert over the 10-20d horizon.
forced_counterparty: short-horizon retail punters and liquidity-motivated benchmark/redemption sellers
failure_mode: Stops working if A-share flow becomes more trend-chasing than mean-reverting, or if steady-turnover names become crowded quality/defensive proxies with no residual underreaction.

name: steady_turnover_relvol_evenness_20
mispricing_source: quiet ownership transfer and slow information diffusion in under-attended names
forced_counterparty: retail attention-chasers and benchmarked funds concentrating liquidity in crowded names
failure_mode: fails in crowding or squeeze regimes where spiky attention is rewarded and quiet participation stops leading price

name: pb_float_crowding_20
mispricing_source: Retail attention and thematic chasing overpay for scarce float in expensive names, while valuation-based arbitrage is slow because it lacks a hard near-term catalyst.
forced_counterparty: Retail theme traders and benchmarked long-only funds that must hold liquid story stocks despite crowded free float.
failure_mode: Fails in liquidity-driven speculative regimes when crowded expensive stocks keep squeezing and valuation does not re-anchor within the 10-20 day horizon.

name: low_lottery_weekly_turnchg_smooth_20
mispricing_source: Attention segmentation and hidden order-splitting; this is a participation-path plus lottery-avoidance signal, not another price-trend or price-volume-correlation clone.
forced_counterparty: retail lottery chasers and benchmark-constrained mutual funds executing inventory transfers without alpha timing
failure_mode: It should weaken in theme-chasing or policy-squeeze regimes where high-lottery names keep trending and smooth turnover simply reflects broad illiquidity rather than accumulation.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_amount_change_stability_20
mispricing_source: Second-order flow-path underreaction; this measures smoothness of weekly capital transfer, not raw momentum, reversal, or plain amount-stability level, so it should be less redundant than another price-volume correlation factor.
forced_counterparty: retail attention traders and benchmark-constrained mutual funds trading around subscriptions, redemptions, and headlines rather than medium-horizon alpha
data_requirements: ["ret_1d","amount"]; for money-ready deployment, add intraday order-flow or member-level trade ledger to verify true absorption vs passive crowding
failure_mode: breaks in one-way policy/speculative momentum regimes where bursty turnover is genuinely informative and return autocorrelation turns positive

name: weekly_float_floorstd_20
mispricing_source: Slow diffusion of information from steady float-normalized sponsorship; the market reacts more to noisy price moves than to persistent non-collapsing flow support.
forced_counterparty: retail attention/lottery traders and benchmarked or redemption-driven long-only funds rotating for non-alpha liquidity reasons
failure_mode: Breaks in policy/thematic momentum regimes where bursty participation is rewarded, or when passive/index flow dominates float turnover so steady support no longer signals informed accumulation.

name: weekly_turnchg_body_stability_20
mispricing_source: Retail attention is drawn to noisy return spikes, not to smooth participation-path information; gradual benchmark-constrained accumulation shows up first in steadier weekly turnover changes and quieter intraday bodies, so this is flow-quality rather than a close-volume price clone.
forced_counterparty: Retail swing traders and liquidity-motivated sellers; institutions that must rebalance gradually instead of taking the whole position at once.
failure_mode: It should stop working in policy-shock or speculative beta-chasing regimes, where orderly low-noise absorption no longer signals latent demand and attention-driven names lead.

name: weekly_turnshock_compress_20_60
mispricing_source: Retail attention bursts create temporary turnover shocks; when those shocks calm, T+1, shorting frictions, and gradual institutional re-risking delay full price adjustment.
forced_counterparty: Retail punters and benchmark-constrained rebalancers trading around attention or policy bursts rather than expected return.
failure_mode: Fails in persistent momentum or policy-driven attention regimes where high turnover shocks reflect real information arrival, not noise unwinding.

name: weekly_float_body_quiet_20
mispricing_source: Slow institutional absorption is not fully impounded because attention-driven traders anchor on exciting price action, not steady float consumption.
forced_counterparty: Retail theme chasers and benchmarked managers needing immediate liquidity; both are uneconomic providers of stock to quiet accumulators.
failure_mode: Fails in speculation-led melt-up regimes where noisy high-body names lead and quiet flow no longer indicates underownership.

name: weekly_volume_evenness_20
mispricing_source: Slow ownership transfer under retail attention bias and T+1 frictions; distinct from existing price-volume correlation factors because it uses only the time-shape of share turnover, not price response.
forced_counterparty: retail attention traders chasing spike-volume names; benchmark/rebalance and redemption-driven funds trading for mandate or liquidity rather than medium-horizon alpha
data_requirements: ["volume"] for research; intraday order-flow or member-level trade ledger is still needed before money-ready deployment to separate accumulation from distribution
failure_mode: Fails when smooth volume mainly reflects stagnant illiquidity, or when policy/speculative regimes reward bursty high-volume leaders so spike suppression becomes a negative.

name: float_weekly_flow_change_stability_20
mispricing_source: patient accumulation is only partially impounded because daily price discovery is dominated by lottery-style attention and short-horizon flow shocks
forced_counterparty: retail hot-money rotators and benchmark/rebalance sellers providing stock mechanically
failure_mode: fails when market-wide theme chasing overwhelms stock-specific flow quality, or when float scarcity stops differentiating names

name: amt_growth_compress_20_60
mispricing_source: attention-driven overreaction in flow, followed by slow institutional absorption and delayed repricing
forced_counterparty: retail attention-chasers and redemption/margin-driven sellers
failure_mode: fails in broad breakout/liquidity-frenzy regimes where expanding flow is the signal and compressed flow just means neglect

name: smallfloat_weekly_flow_compress_20_60
mispricing_source: The market reacts to visible price moves faster than to the quieter information in shrinking weekly trading shocks; once bursty flow normalizes, prices often lag the clearing of supply overhang.
forced_counterparty: Retail theme chasers and redemption-constrained small funds who trade in bursts; benchmarked long-only managers are slow to add after the flow regime has already improved.
failure_mode: Fails in broad speculative chase regimes where persistent high weekly flow shocks keep being rewarded, or in illiquid small-cap droughts where low recent shock just means neglect.

name: turnover_kurtosis_quiet_20
mispricing_source: episodic attention and speculative churn create temporary over-ownership in spiky-turnover names, while quiet-holder names underreact and reprice gradually
forced_counterparty: short-horizon retail punters and liquidity-seeking benchmarked funds that crowd into high-attention names and neglect quiet ones
failure_mode: fails in retail-mania or breakout regimes where turnover spikes reflect persistent information leadership rather than temporary crowding

name: smallfloat_turnshock_compress_20_60
mispricing_source: Underreaction to post-churn float lock-up; retail attention fades after noisy turnover episodes, but the supply/demand improvement persists into the 10-20 day horizon.
forced_counterparty: Retail churners, fund-redemption sellers, and benchmark/rebalance-driven sellers who must supply stock during consolidation regardless of near-term alpha.
failure_mode: Fails when turnover compression reflects dead liquidity or broad small-cap de-risking rather than genuine strong-hand absorption.

name: anti_autocorr_weekly_relvol_change_20
mispricing_source: Retail attention overshoots create bursty abnormal-volume paths; negative weekly autocorrelation in those shocks is a participation-path signal, not a simple price-trend clone.
forced_counterparty: short-horizon retail flow chasers and liquidity-sensitive managers who only add after persistent volume confirmation
failure_mode: This should stop working in persistent speculation regimes where positive attention autocorrelation is rewarded and volume bursts keep compounding instead of mean-reverting.

name: amtchg_turnfloor_compress_20_60
mispricing_source: Slow repricing after liquidity-shock absorption; the signal comes from flow-path compression plus a persistent participation floor, not from a recycled price-momentum variant.
forced_counterparty: Retail churners and redemption-driven funds that sell or withdraw liquidity once excitement fades, leaving patient accumulators to absorb supply gradually.
failure_mode: Fails when lower flow volatility reflects true information vacuum or policy-induced trading suppression rather than absorbed supply, or when headline momentum makes price react immediately to flow.
